CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Sep-09-2024 Continued To Nov-27-24 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Defendant City And County Of San Francisco?S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Declare Plaintiff A Vexatious Litigant And Request For Prefiling Order And Order Requiring Plaintiff To Furnish Security
Off Calendar Sep-03-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION TO DECLARE PLAINTIFF A VEXATIOUS LITIGANT AND REQUEST FOR PREFILING ORDER AND ORDER REQUIRING PLAINTIFF TO FURNISH SECURITY.

No appearances. The Court adopts its tentative ruling: GRANTED as follows:
1. Defendant's Motion to Declare Plaintiff a Vexatious Litigant is GRANTED. Under California Code of Civil Procedure (CCP) 391(b), a vexatious litigant is defined as a pro per plaintiff who filed at least five litigations within the last seven years which have been finally determined adversely to them. Plaintiff has filed in-excess of the minimum five required cases adversely decided against him within the past seven years to meet the standard of a vexatious litigant.
2. Defendant's request for an order requiring Plaintiff to furnish security in the amount of $25,000 is GRANTED. Upon finding that the Plaintiff is a vexatious litigant, the Court shall order the vexatious litigant to furnish security in the amount it deems appropriate per CCP 391.3.
3. Defendant's request for a prefiling order under CCP 391.7(a) is GRANTED. To file any future pro per litigations, Plaintiff must first obtain leave from the presiding judge. Violation of this order may be punishable by contempt of court.
4. The Court takes judicial notice of Defendant's 82 Exhibits in support of their motion. The Court may take judicial notice of a document's legal effect and where there is only one reasonable interpretation of a document. The Court takes judicial notice of the existence of prior litigation filed by the Plaintiff and the adverse results.
Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order. Judge: Charles F. Haines, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (501/CFH)
